question_text: Which statement correctly describes how particles are arranged in a solid?

options.label: A
options.text: Particles are tightly packed in fixed positions and vibrate in place

options.label: B
options.text: Particles are far apart and move randomly throughout the space

options.label: C
options.text: Particles are loosely packed and slide past each other easily

options.label: D
options.text: Particles have no forces between them and move at high speed

correct_answer: A) Particles are tightly packed in fixed positions and vibrate in place
explanation: In solids, particles are arranged closely together in a fixed, ordered pattern and can only vibrate around their fixed positions. This explains why solids have a definite shape and volume. Options B, C and D describe gases or liquids incorrectly.
